title: gomphostigma incomptum
intro: Gomphostigma incomptum, also known as swamp hydrangea or water button, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily as a diuretic and to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant contains bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used topically as a poultice or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be employed for therapeutic purposes.
